questions.question: What is the generation-order problem that doomed earlier autoregressive image models?
options: GPUs process pixels in random order

Pixels/tokens live on a 2D grid but AR models visit them in 1D raster order — an early corner pixel has no idea what the image eventually becomes, so quality scaled worse than GPT-on-text and never matched diffusion at equal compute
Images have no natural ordering at all
Raster order is too fast to learn from
questions.explanation: PixelCNN went pixel-by-pixel; DALL-E 1 and Parti went token-by-token over VQ codes. Both inherited the same flawed 1D ordering of inherently 2D data.

questions.question: How does VAR change what is being predicted?
options: It predicts pixels in spiral order

Next-SCALE prediction: predict a 1x1 token (image summary), then 2x2, then 4x4, up to the full grid — each scale conditions causally on all previous scales while all positions WITHIN a scale are predicted in parallel
It predicts the image caption first
It predicts noise like diffusion
questions.explanation: The order problem disappears: the whole image at scale k arrives in one transformer pass, coarse-to-fine, like progressive JPEG rendering but generative.

questions.question: How does the multi-scale VQ tokenizer work?
options: A separate codebook per resolution

One shared codebook (4096-16384 codes) tokenizes the latent at every scale, trained so the upsampled scale embeddings SUM to reconstruct the latent — a residual-VQ variant where scale k captures what scales 1..k−1 missed
JPEG coefficients become tokens
Each scale crops a different region
questions.explanation: Trained once like VQGAN, then frozen — all generative work happens in the autoregressive transformer on top, exactly mirroring the two-stage latent-diffusion recipe.

questions.question: What did the VAR paper demonstrate about scaling?
options: Scaling laws don't apply to images
VAR matches GPT-style scaling laws for image generation — predictable quality gains with compute — and beats DiT at the same compute budget
VAR only works below 64x64
It scales but loses to PixelCNN
questions.explanation: That's the headline: the predictable-scaling property that made AR dominate language finally transferred to images once the generation order respected image structure.

questions.question: How does VAR's attention structure differ from a standard GPT?
options: It uses no attention at all

Causality applies in SCALE order, not token order: a token at scale k attends to all tokens at scales 1..k, position embeddings encode both scale index and spatial position, and intra-scale prediction is fully parallel
Every token attends only to neighbors
Attention is replaced by convolution
questions.explanation: The loss is still GPT-style cross-entropy on discrete codes — the sequence is just scale-structured rather than linearly ordered, which is the entire trick.
